title: Bound session context to a secondary runtime role
plan_sections: Server-owned transaction variables vs client-managed inputs
goal: Restrict session-related behavior so session_id remains useful for observability, correlation, handoff continuity, and helper-call continuity, but does not control canonical transaction identity, active transaction selection, or deterministic resume correctness.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 redesign explicitly demotes session context from a correctness anchor to a bounded secondary role. This ticket ensures the runtime implementation follows that model and removes any remaining session-primary behavior from begin, resume, identity, and active-transaction logic.
scope: Review all runtime code paths that read, derive, store, recover, or branch on session_id.
Identify any places where session context currently influences active transaction identity, active transaction selection, begin/resume decisions, or deterministic continuation.
Retain session usage only where it serves observability, correlation, handoff continuity, or helper-call continuity.
Update runtime behavior so session-derived values cannot override exact canonical transaction identity or the minimal canonical continuation contract.
Document any bounded historical compatibility behavior involving older session-oriented assumptions.
out_of_scope: Redesigning the canonical transaction identity model itself.
Redesigning materialized-state schema beyond what is needed to remove session-primary behavior.
Broad compatibility rewrites unrelated to session semantics.
Adding the full interruption-focused regression suite.
inputs: Outputs from p0-t01, p0-t02, and p0-t03
Runtime modules that persist or recover session context
Runtime modules that select active transactions or dispatch resume behavior
rules: session_id is secondary runtime context and not part of canonical runtime identity.
session_id must not determine active transaction identity.
session_id must not determine active transaction selection.
session_id must not determine deterministic resume correctness.
Recovered session_id may be used only for observability, correlation, handoff continuity, or helper-call continuity.
If session-related historical compatibility logic is retained, it must not weaken exact active-transaction continuation under 0.6.0 semantics.
expected_outputs: A map of all session-related code paths in the runtime.
A list of places where session_id currently acts or could act as a correctness anchor.
Runtime changes that remove session-primary behavior from active transaction and resume logic.
A short implementation note documenting the bounded role of session context after the change.
acceptance_criteria: The runtime no longer uses session_id as the basis for active transaction identity or active transaction selection.
Deterministic resume is driven by canonical transaction identity and the minimal canonical continuation contract, not by session-parent or session-first reasoning.
Recovered session_id values remain available where needed for observability, correlation, handoff continuity, or helper compatibility.
Any retained session-related compatibility handling is explicitly bounded to historical behavior and does not alter strict new runtime semantics.
The resulting behavior matches the plan: session context is secondary and transaction-centered protocol logic remains primary.
review_checkpoints: Confirm that no begin/resume path prefers session_id over active_tx, tx_id, status, or next_action.
Confirm that helper continuity still works where session_id is legitimately needed.
Confirm that observability and correlation outputs remain available after removing session-primary control flow.
notes: This ticket should remove session-primary logic rather than add new explanatory metadata.
If the current implementation mixes session context with active transaction lookup, prefer exact active-transaction continuation and bounded compatibility over preserving legacy convenience behavior.
This ticket operationalizes the plan's requirement that transaction-centered correctness takes priority over session hierarchy.
